name: Cairo

palette.name: Desert Limestone
palette.rationale: Warm limestone and desert light form the most legible neutral base for Cairo.

palette.name: Nile Blue
palette.rationale: A deep river blue anchors the city to the Nile corridor and waterfront life.

palette.name: Cairene Dust
palette.rationale: Ochre-brown captures weathered masonry, dust, and warm urban fabric.

palette.name: Museum Gold
palette.rationale: Muted gold references pharaonic artifacts and museum collections without becoming a flag claim.

palette.name: Flag Black
palette.rationale: Black comes from Egyptian national-symbol context visible in the capital.

palette.name: Flag Red
palette.rationale: Red comes from Egyptian national-symbol context and is not a Cairo-specific official color.

palette.name: Mosque Stone
palette.rationale: A light stone tone supports historic mosque courtyards, facades, and sunlit streets.

sources.title: Tourism
sources.note: Used for official city tourism context.

sources.title: Historic Cairo
sources.note: Used for historic urban and architectural context.

notes: Complete starter record; national flag colors are symbolic official context, while material and landscape colors are editorial.
editorialSummary: Cairo's complete palette layers desert limestone, Nile blue, ochre dust, museum gold, Egyptian symbolic red and black, and mosque-stone neutrals.
